summary: Construct an Agent record with empty messages, ready for repeated step calls. :api-key and :max-tokens are auto-injected into provider-options. :convert-to-llm projects custom AgentMessages onto canonical Messages before each provider call.

Run one user turn. Appends a UserMessage, then iterates provider-call -> tool-execution until a non-tool stop reason or the safety cap. Cooperative yields when called inside a coroutine; ?cancel-fn polled at every yield.
Hard ceiling on tool-call iterations per step. Bump if a real workflow needs more, don't remove.
Dispatch ev to handlers[ev.type] and the `:*` wildcard bucket.
Subscribe handler to event-name. Owner-tagged handlers are removed by unregister-by-owner.
Register a contribution under the given kind. See contracts.register-kinds for the kind list.
Look up and pcall-isolate a registered slash command. Emits :error on failure.
Contribute a system-prompt fragment. text-or-fn is a string or a (ctx)->string function. opts may carry :id :title :description :order.
Render all registered prompt fragments into one string, joined by blank lines, ordered by :order then registration order.
Append registered :tool contributions to base, preserving order. Duplicates last-wins on tool name.
Run all :before-tool hooks against the pending call. Hooks may inspect or replace args.
Drop every contribution and event handler tagged with owner. Used by the loader and by reloadable modules at the top of their bodies.

List registered contributions of a given kind. kind is one of :tools :commands :controls :status :panels :presenters :providers :auth-backends :session-backends :extensions :event-handlers :prompt-fragments.
Look up a provider by its registered :name.
Find the first provider whose :api matches. Many providers can share an :api family.
All providers registered for the given :api family.
Look up an auth backend by its registered :name.
Look up a session backend by its registered :name.
Activate a registered session backend by name. Subsequent appends route through it.
Return the active session backend record, or nil if --no-session is in effect.
Cache the SessionInfo returned by a backend's :start! for later inspection.
Return the session info cached by the active backend.
Wipe all registries in place so identity references (e.g. presenter ui-slot) survive reset.

Return the small stable api table handed to an extension. Carries owner-scoped wrappers around register / on / emit / prompt / list, plus the version field and a presenter ui-slot. This is the public extension contract.
Compatibility helper for in-process callers/tests. Prefer (extensions.register :provider provider owner) in extensions.
Resolve a provider by registered :name. Errors if the name is unknown.

Dispatch a completion to the named provider. Returns a canonical AssistantMessage. The provider chooses native streaming, cooperative-yield streaming, or blocking based on which callbacks are present.
Synthesize streaming block events from a complete AssistantMessage. Compatibility bridge for providers that do not implement :complete-stream natively.
Current epoch in milliseconds. Used as the :timestamp field on canonical messages.
Build a {:type :text :text s} block. The visible-text content kind.
Build a {:type :thinking ...} block. Carries reasoning text plus the opaque echo signature required by Anthropic extended thinking and OpenAI Responses for multi-turn echo.
Build a {:type :tool-call :id :name :arguments} block. Arguments is a parsed Lua table — providers JSON-decode wire arguments before calling this.
Build a {:role :user :content :timestamp} message. content is a string or [TextContent].

signature: (assistant-message {: content : api : provider : model : usage : stop-reason : error-message}) -> AssistantMessage
summary: Build a canonical AssistantMessage. Content defaults to []; usage and stop-reason fall back to safe defaults; error-message is set only when provided.

signature: (tool-result-message {: tool-call-id : tool-name : content : details : is-error?}) -> ToolResultMessage
summary: Build a canonical ToolResultMessage. content is always an array; details is opaque presenter payload.
Build an AssistantMessage representing a transport/HTTP failure. Sets stop-reason :error and inserts a synthetic "[error] ..." text block.
Concatenate every TextContent block in msg.content. Returns "" if there are no text blocks.
Return every :tool-call block in msg.content, in source order.
Return every :thinking block in msg.content, in source order.
Auth credential backend. Resolves an api-key or rotates an OAuth token for one or more providers.
Slash command contribution. Looked up by name when the user submits `/<name> <args>` from a presenter.
Keyboard/UI control surface for presenters that support typed input bindings.
Lifecycle hook (currently `before-tool`). Inspects/replaces a tool call before it executes.
Non-modal side panel contribution rendered by presenters that support panels.
UI driver. Owns the input/output loop. Exactly one is active per run; the loader picks based on flags and manifest hints.
LLM provider contribution. See the :provider-interface contract for the required record.
Persistence backend for canonical messages. The `--session` flag selects one and `core.extensions.set-active-session-backend!` activates it.
Status-line contributor — produces a short string for the presenter's status row.
System-prompt fragment. Either a static string or `(ctx) -> string`. Ordered by `:order` (default 0); rendered fragments are joined with blank lines.
Agent tool contribution. Merged into the per-step `AgentContext.tools` and dispatched by name when the assistant emits a ToolCall.
Emitted once per run during teardown. `:error` is present for crashed paths.
Emitted once per run after setup and before the first new step.
Stream finished. Emitted once after all per-block end events for a single AssistantMessage.
Final visible text emitted by the assistant. One per AssistantMessage with text blocks.
Streaming text token. Aggregated by presenters during a stream.
Final reasoning text from the assistant (for providers that surface reasoning content).
Streaming reasoning token.
Cooperative cancel observed; the current step appended an aborted assistant message.
Presenter signal that an open overlay/picker should close.
Provider stream: terminal event for a successful AssistantMessage.
Generic surface error — typically command-dispatch or user-input failures.
Emitted when an extension event handler raises. Suppressed for recursive extension-error events to prevent loops.
Emitted by the loader for each successfully loaded extension manifest.
Transient informational message intended for the presenter status row or panel.
Provider call completed (success or error). The :message field carries the canonical AssistantMessage.
Provider call beginning.
Emitted by `fen.core.agent` immediately after `agent.messages` grows.
User-line queued while the agent is busy. Consumed by the agent on natural-stop.
Presenter hint that a registered panel needs to be repainted.
Presenter signal that the UI should be torn down and re-initialized (used by /reload).
Presenter signal that the active conversation should be cleared (used by /new).
Presenter-level status hint. Owners post a transient status line; nil clears.
Provider stream opened. Marker event emitted before any block events.
Provider stream: incremental text token within the open block.
Provider stream: TextContent block closed; full text supplied.
Provider stream: a TextContent block is starting.
Provider stream: incremental reasoning token within the open block.
Provider stream: ThinkingContent block closed; full text supplied.
Provider stream: a ThinkingContent block is starting.
Tool call about to execute. Carries the canonical ToolCall block.
Provider stream: incremental JSON-text fragment for the open ToolCall arguments. Some providers stream arguments token-by-token; consumers may concatenate.
Provider stream: ToolCall block closed; complete canonical ToolCall block supplied.
Provider stream: a ToolCall block is starting; arguments not yet known.
Tool execution finished. Carries the canonical ToolResultMessage.
User-submitted line accepted by the presenter input layer. Distinct from :queued (which fires when the line is *queued* during a busy turn).
Per-call payload handed to a provider's `:complete`.
Tool extended with execution metadata for the agent loop. Registered through `(api.register :tool ...)`.
Outcome of a tool execution.
Single model response. Content is always an array, even when empty.
Union of UserMessage, AssistantMessage, ToolResultMessage. Stored on `agent.messages` and passed to providers in `AgentContext.messages`.
Why the assistant stopped producing output.
Plain visible text block.
Reasoning/extended-thinking block. Surfaces both Anthropic extended thinking and OpenAI reasoning items.
Provider-agnostic tool spec — what providers see in `AgentContext.tools`.
Assistant request to invoke a tool. Arguments are a parsed Lua table — providers JSON-decode wire arguments before constructing this block.
Result of a single tool call, carried back to the provider on the next turn.
Token usage counters returned by the provider (best-effort — providers fill what they can).
Single user turn. Content is either a plain string or an array of TextContent blocks.
Required record shape for `(api.register :auth-backend ...)`.
Required record shape for `(api.register :provider ...)`. See the :provider register-kind for field details.
Required record shape for `(api.register :session-backend ...)`.

description: Read structured state of the running agent. Read-only; does not evaluate code. Query is a tiny Fennel-shaped data language. Examples: (:get :model), (:count (:get :messages)), (:get :messages -1), (:pluck (:get :tools) :name), (:get :extensions :panels), (:where (:get :messages) :role :assistant), (:last (:where (:get :messages) :role :assistant)), (:slice (:get :messages) -5 5), (:keys (:get)). Prefer narrow queries over dumping large roots. Output defaults to JSON; use format=fennel for Fennel rendering when available.
name: agent_state

description: Reload one external extension by name
name: reload-extension

description: Pick an extension and show its detail panel
name: extensions

description: Show available commands and controls
name: help

description: Switch model (overlay if no arg; index/name/substring if given)
name: model

description: Toggle the prompt-fragments panel; /prompt rendered emits the rendered prompt
name: prompt

description: Toggle the queue panel; /queue clear|mode preserve their actions
name: queue

description: Cancel current turn and clear queues
name: cancel-all

description: Reset the current conversation and start a fresh session
name: new

description: Hot-reload core modules and source overlays
name: reload

description: Alias for /new
name: n

description: Pick a recent session to resume (overlay)
name: sessions

description: Resume a session (overlay if no arg; id/prefix/path/index if given)
name: resume

description: Alias for /reload
name: r

description: Toggle the status panel (model, provider, tokens, session)
name: status

description: Summarize this session, seed a fresh session with the summary
name: handoff

description: Toggle the memory diagnostics panel; /mem gc forces a GC pass
name: mem

description: ChatGPT subscription PKCE OAuth credentials shared with the Codex CLI.
name: openai-codex

description: Append-only JSONL session backend under XDG state. Records canonical messages, replayable via --continue / /resume.
name: jsonl

description: Toggle tool-result bodies
name: toggle-tool-results

description: Toggle thinking blocks
name: toggle-thinking-blocks

description: Quit; ctrl-c also clears input or cancels a busy turn
name: quit

description: Toggle full vs collapsed tool-result bodies
name: expand

description: Toggle Markdown rendering of assistant text
name: markdown

description: Show or hide assistant thinking blocks
name: thinking

description: Web presenter spinner row shown while the agent is busy.
name: busy
